news_title: Product safety recall - 2020 - 2027 Land Rover Defender, Discovery and Range Rover vehicles - The National Consumer Commission (NCC)
efet_title: Product safety recall - 2020 - 2027 Land Rover Defender, Discovery and Range Rover vehicles - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Product safety recall – 2020 – 2027 Land Rover Defender, Discovery and Range Rover vehicles
Date Posted:
June 29, 2026
The National Consumer Commission (NCC) informs consumers of a product recall issued by Jaguar Land Rover South Africa (Pty) Ltd affecting selected 2020 to 2027 model year Defender, Discovery and Range Rover vehicles. The affected vehicles were sold nationally during the following periods:
Defender: April 2019 to June 2026
Discovery: July 2020 to June 2026
Range Rover: September 2020 to June 2026
According to the supplier, a concern has been identified with the connector to the driver’s airbag at the clockspring. Over time, the connector may experience fretting corrosion, which can increase resistance in the driver’s airbag circuit. This may result in the driver’s airbag failing to deploy when required to do so.
Consumers who own the affected vehicles are urged to visit their nearest Jaguar Land Rover dealership to arrange for an inspection and repair. Any remedial work will be carried out at no cost to the consumer.
Questions relating to this recall may be directed to:
ProductRecall@thencc.org.za
.
Ends
Issued by: National Consumer Commission (NCC)
For media enquiries, contact: Phetho Ntaba (Spokesperson)
082 809 2031/012 065 2040

efet_title: Product Recalls: Tissue Box Toy and Batteries of a Santa Cruz Heckler 9EBIKE - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Product Recalls: Tissue Box Toy and Batteries of a Santa Cruz Heckler 9EBIKE
Date Posted:
May 7, 2025
The National Consumer Commission (NCC) provides information on the recall of the lithium-ion batteries of Santa Cruz Heckler 9 ebikes and the Tissue Box Toy sold with the Fisher-Price 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers, as notified by the suppliers.

news_title: Product safety recalls - Ford Transit and Ford Tourneo Custom and Jeep Grand Cherokee vehicles - The National Consumer Commission (NCC)
efet_title: Product safety recalls - Ford Transit and Ford Tourneo Custom and Jeep Grand Cherokee vehicles - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Product safety recalls – Ford Transit and Ford Tourneo Custom and Jeep Grand Cherokee vehicles
Date Posted:
June 25, 2026
The National Consumer Commission (NCC) notifies consumers about the recalls of certain Ford Transit and Ford Tourneo Custom, as well as Jeep Grand Cherokee vehicles, as reported by the respective suppliers.
FORD TRANSIT AND FORD TOURNEO CUSTOM VEHICLES
Ford Motor Company of Southern Africa (Pty) Ltd informed the NCC that they are recalling certain six hundred and thirty-three (633) 2025 to 2026 model year Ford Transit and Ford Tourneo Custom models that were sold nationally, between 1 March 2025 and 20 May 2026. The recall is due to a fault that may cause either gradual deterioration in wiper function or a sudden, complete loss of operation. This condition may reduce or eliminate windshield visibility under certain driving conditions, increasing the risk of an accident.
JEEP GRAND CHEROKEE VEHICLES
Stellantis South Africa (Pty) Ltd informed the NCC that it is recalling eighty-two (82) Jeep Grand Cherokee models that were made available from 2021 to 2023. According to the supplier, the rear coil spring may not have been correctly installed. The rear coil spring may detach from the vehicle while driving, causing a hazard to drivers and occupants of other vehicles. The vehicle may crash without prior warning and/or may result in injury to other road users.
Consumers who own these models are urged to visit their nearest authorised dealership for inspection and the necessary repair work, which will be carried out at no cost to the consumers.
Questions relating to this recall may be directed to:
ProductRecall@thencc.org.za
.
Ends
Issued by: National Consumer Commission (NCC)
For media enquiries, contact: Phetho Ntaba (Spokesperson)

efet_title: Media Statement: Deli Hummus range Product Safety Recall - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Media Statement: Deli Hummus range Product Safety Recall
Date Posted:
October 3, 2024
On 16 September 2024, Shoprite Checkers notified the Consumer Commission (NCC) of a product recall of the Deli Hummus range manufactured by BM Foods (a member of Sea Harvest Group), following the detection of Listeria Monocytogenes which is a species of foodborne bacteria.
The affected products are the Deli Hummus range, specifically the following:
Traditional Hummus (125g and 300g);
Reduced Fat Hummus (125g);
Zataar Hummus (125g and 300g);
Red Pepper Hummus (125g and 300g); and
Caramelised Onion Hummus (125g).
The respective sell-by dates of the products are 10 September 2024 to 08 October 2024.
Subsequent to the product recall notification, and in line with Section 60 of the Consumer Protection Act 68 of 2008, Shoprite Checkers has now provided an interim Investigation Report to the NCC on the Deli Hummus range. The preliminary findings of the report point to some areas of concern on product handling at the manufacturing level, however, the NCC awaits a final report from Shoprite Checkers. Parallel to the said in-house processes, the NCC is independently investigating the recalled products on identified operational issues that pertain to food safety.
The NCC’s Acting Commissioner, Mr. Hardin Ratshisusu, urged consumers to immediately stop consuming the products and return same to the supplier for a full refund with or without any proof of purchase. He further added that “at this stage, the NCC is not able to determine the extent of the distribution of the products as this is the subject of an investigation. Suppliers are urged not to distribute these products further until the investigation is finalised.”
The NCC is monitoring this recall based on the Product Recall provisions of the Consumer Protection Action read with the product recall safety guidelines.
ENDS
Issued by: National Consumer Commission (NCC)
